mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening economic output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during easing periods or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero (p > 0.10) across multiple easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Market absorbs inflation shocks without drawdowns, resulting in positive 20-day returns in >65% of threshold-crossing events despite rising price pressures.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 or yields negative returns during confirmed expansion phases over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly correlates with increased corporate earnings capacity, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently decouple from equity performance, producing a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 5-year windows despite strong macro growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals a collapsing construction sector and reduced household wealth formation, which dampens short-term market momentum and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing market downturns fail to transmit to equity markets, with 5-day forward returns remaining positive in >70% of severe contraction events.
